blocks.content: Kit's flight landed at Hartsfield on a Friday afternoon. His mom was waiting at arrivals in the blue Honda, and when he got in the car she looked at him for a long moment before she put it in drive.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: Four months can be a long time.
The drive to Millhaven took two hours. He watched Georgia come back through the window — red clay and pine trees and the particular flatness of home — and felt something in his chest loosen.
His room was exactly as he'd left it. He dropped his bag and sat on the edge of the bed and sent one text.
one more day. my last final is tomorrow morning

He lay back on his bed. The ceiling had the same water stain in the corner from when a pipe leaked during seventh grade. He'd been staring at that stain his whole life. It occurred to him that he hadn't thought about it once in four months.

His mom knocked at eight with dinner and they ate at the kitchen table and she asked about USC and the film and Darius and she didn't ask about Daisy — but he knew she was waiting for him to bring it up, and he didn't, and she let it sit.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: The Joneses' lights were on when I drove past Tuesday. Her mom mentioned she gets back tomorrow.
His mom smiled at her plate. The small private one.
